The following MySQL stored procedure removes inactive users from the users table while logging changes to status_log.
It begins by declaring variables to capture the user_id and current status before deletion.
A transaction block ensures data consistency across both the deletion and the logging steps.
Error handling is implemented using a handler to catch any SQL exceptions that might occur.
If an error arises, the transaction rolls back and the specific error message is returned to the caller.
The procedure iterates through records with an Inactive status to process them individually.
Each record is first logged into the status_log table with the current timestamp.
After successful logging, the corresponding row is deleted from the main users table.
This approach guarantees that no data is lost without a corresponding audit trail entry.
The total count of affected rows is calculated to verify the operation's scope.
total records processed successfully are reported upon completion of the loop.
The procedure concludes by committing the transaction if no errors were encountered.
You can call this stored procedure whenever you need to clean up inactive accounts.
